death.note: d. age 66, in his sleep, at his Newburyport home.

marriages.notes: m. April 17, 1937 NYC Adelaide Ferry Hooker (H04640). Divorced 1958. 3 children (not built per Hooker-centric scope — non-Hooker bloodline through father).

notable.notable_blurb: Pulitzer Prize-winning novelist; The Late George Apley

narrative_blocks.header: Snubbed by Harvard, satirized them for decades
narrative_blocks.body: As a public-school scholarship boy in the Gold Coast era he was rejected by the Harvard Crimson. Forty years of New England class novels followed, including the Pulitzer-winning Late George Apley.

narrative_blocks.header: Mr. Moto novels ran from 1935 to 1957
narrative_blocks.body: Alongside his literary novels he wrote the Mr. Moto Japanese-detective series, six books published 1935-1957. Several were adapted into films starring Peter Lorre as the inscrutable Japanese investigator.

narrative_blocks.header: Married Adelaide Ferry Hooker — Rockefeller in-law
narrative_blocks.body: He married Adelaide Ferry Hooker in 1937 — the year his Pulitzer-winning Late George Apley was published. Her sister Blanchette married John D. Rockefeller III, placing Marquand alongside the Rockefeller family until his 1958 divorce.

narrative_blocks.header: Cousin of Buckminster Fuller via Margaret
narrative_blocks.body: His mother Margaret Fuller Marquand was a great-niece of the transcendentalist Margaret Fuller. The geodesic-dome inventor was his cousin.

narrative_blocks.header: Died in his sleep at Newburyport home
narrative_blocks.body: Heart attack on July 16, 1960, age 66. Buried at Sawyer Hill Burying Ground; his second wife Adelaide joined him three years later.

burial.plot_notes: Sawyer Hill Burying Ground, Newburyport MA — buried with second wife Adelaide Ferry Hooker Marquand (H04640) who died 3 years later.

education.notes: Public-school graduate at the height of Harvard's Gold Coast era. Treated as 'unclubbable outsider.' Turned down by Harvard Crimson, elected to editorial board of Harvard Lampoon. Family had long Harvard tradition.

career.notes: Reporter, later on bi-weekly magazine section.

Won Pulitzer Prize 1938 for 'The Late George Apley.' Mr. Moto spy series 1935-1957 (8 motion pictures). Time Magazine 1944: most successful novelist in US. 16+ literary novels. WWII war correspondent. Yale collection: YCAL MSS 48.

military_service.notes: Joined Battery A while a Harvard student. Activated July 1916 + sent to Mexican border with Pershing Punitive Expedition.

military_service.unit: U.S. Army (continued from Massachusetts National Guard activation)
military_service.notes: Engaged in combat in France with many of his Harvard classmates. Pre-war Mexican Border Service preceded WWI service.

death.note: d. age 66, in his sleep, at his Newburyport home.

marriages.notes: m. April 17, 1937 NYC Adelaide Ferry Hooker (H04640). Divorced 1958. 3 children (not built per Hooker-centric scope — non-Hooker bloodline through father).

Won Pulitzer Prize 1938 for 'The Late George Apley.' Mr. Moto spy series 1935-1957 (8 motion pictures). Time Magazine 1944: most successful novelist in US. 16+ literary novels. WWII war correspondent. Yale collection: YCAL MSS 48.

military_service.notes: Engaged in combat in France with many of his Harvard classmates. Pre-war Mexican Border Service preceded WWI service.

death.note: Cremated at Mount Auburn Cemetery (CEM110) on October 14, 1963; remains interred at Sawyer Hill Burying Ground Newburyport (with husband John Phillips Marquand).

marriages.notes: 3 children with John P. Marquand. Children not built per Hooker-centric scope (Marquand-Hooker line; non-Hooker bloodline through father).

sources.short: Blanchette H. Rockefeller papers (RAC) — Hooker and Ferry Family Files Series 53/1

narrative_blocks.header: Russia trip with sister Helen Hooker, 1928
narrative_blocks.body: In 1928, at age 25, Adelaide Ferry Hooker embarked with her younger sister Helen (then 23) on an extended journey through Germany, Sweden, Finland, and the Soviet Union — six months of which were spent in the USSR while Helen studied with avant-garde painter Pavel Filonov in Leningrad. On return in spring 1929, Adelaide wrote a series of articles on their Russian travels for Good Housekeeping, illustrated by Helen — a collaboration between two literary-artistic Hooker daughters. Adelaide later married novelist John Phillips Marquand in 1937, the year his Pulitzer-winning The Late George Apley was published; they had three children before divorcing in 1958.

burial.plot_notes: Sawyer Hill Burying Ground, Newburyport MA. Cremated at Mount Auburn (CEM110) Oct 14, 1963 then interred at Sawyer Hill with husband John Phillips Marquand.

bio.extra_names: Bears the full name of his maternal grandfather Elon Huntington Hooker (H03434) — the only great-grandchild in this Hooker line to inherit the patriarch's complete given name as personal first + middle names. The naming preserves the Hooker family identifier across the Marquand line.

death.cause: cardiac (in cardiac ICU at Maine Medical Center)
death.note: d. age 77, 'unexpectedly,' with wife Jean at his side, peacefully in cardiac ICU.

burial.plot_notes: Dennis Village Cemetery, Cape Cod MA. Burial private.

marriages.notes: Married Jean MacMurtry (X02336, knew her many years before marriage) at Dennis Union Church on Cape Cod late in life. After marriage moved to East Orleans Cape Cod, then wintered in Aiken SC with their horses, then 10 years before his death moved to Pownal Maine ('The Sanctuary'). No children documented in obituary.

education.notes: Per Findagrave obituary — therapeutic boarding school for adolescents with mental health challenges.

narrative_blocks.header: Bore his Hooker grandfather's full given name
narrative_blocks.body: Adelaide named him Elon Huntington Hooker Marquand in 1943. He was the only great-grandchild given the patriarch's complete first and middle names.

narrative_blocks.header: Both parents died when he was a teenager
narrative_blocks.body: His father John Marquand died in 1960 when Lonny was seventeen. Adelaide followed three years later, and he spent his life looking for somewhere to call home.

narrative_blocks.header: Returned to Cape Cod in his final years
narrative_blocks.body: Bipolar diagnosis shaped his middle decades after his parents' early deaths. He died at the Dennis house in 2021, age seventy-seven.

career.notes: Underwrote the Black Mountain Music Festival the year it was beamed via Mountain Stage into the Former Soviet Union from Asheville NC. Devotee of authentic American 'classical' folk music.

naming_inspiration.description: Bears the complete given name of his maternal grandfather Elon Huntington Hooker, founder of the Hooker Electrochemical Company. The only great-grandchild in this Hooker line to inherit the patriarch's full first + middle names as a personal name.

burialCemetery.name: Sawyer Hill Burying Ground
burialCemetery.notes: Historic burying ground in Newburyport MA, Marquand family burial site. Ground used by Newburyport Hooker descendants and the Marquand family lineage. Both John Phillips Marquand (X02321, d.1960) + his wife Adelaide Ferry Hooker Marquand (H04640, d.1963) buried here.

INST022.primary_name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.notes: First American foreign-mission organization. Tree connections: Rev. Elias Cornelius (agent 1816, secretary 1831-32); Ainsworth and Harriet Ellsworth Blunt, missionaries to the Cherokee at Brainerd, Tennessee; Eliza Talcott, missionary to Japan; Rev. William Banfield Capron, Madura mission; Judge John Hooker, member.
